question_text: A student heats a solid metal block. The particles vibrate more vigorously, but the block does not change state. Which statement best explains why the volume of the block increases slightly?

options.label: A
options.text: New particles are created as the metal is heated

options.label: B
options.text: The particles move faster and need more space, pushing against each other and spreading apart

options.label: C
options.text: The particles become larger as they absorb heat energy

options.label: D
options.text: The attractive forces between particles become stronger when heated

correct_answer: The particles move faster and need more space, pushing against each other and spreading apart
explanation: When a solid is heated, its particles vibrate with greater amplitude. Although particles in solids stay in fixed positions, increased vibration means they need more average space around them. This causes the solid to expand slightly in volume—a process called thermal expansion. The particles don't move freely like in gases, but their more vigorous vibrations push them slightly further apart on average.
